diary:
Sunny Monday had that soft early-June glow, and I felt it even before I stepped out. Woke up at 5:30-ish at 5305 Island Crest Way on Mercer Island, made coffee/espresso, and packed my visit bag like I always do—snacks, paperwork, the little things that keep me steady. Quick stretches at the Mercer Island Community Center, then Whole Foods for a to-go breakfast and a second espresso for the road.

Issaquah was busy in that quiet hospice way: home assessments, symptom checks, family support notes—listening more than talking, keeping my voice calm. After lunch at QFC and some paperwork time, I finally had a breather heading back toward Mercer Island. Later I grabbed fly-fishing line/tippet at REI in Bellevue, then changed at home and checked the match schedule.

Downtown at Pike Place Market felt like the whole city leaned in for USA vs Wales. I watched at The Whale Wins with my friend—laughing between plays, rooting hard when it mattered. After Caffé Vita on Capitol Hill and a pickup game at Bellevue Downtown Park, I drove home, showered, set tomorrow’s gear, and read until my eyes got heavy. I’m tired, but in a good way—like I actually showed up today.
thought_process: I planned my day around momentum and recovery: morning routine first (coffee + gear), then light movement at the Mercer Island Community Center so I wouldn’t feel stiff before the visits. Issaquah work took over the clock naturally—assessments and symptom checks always require a slower, measured pace, so I tried not to rush my transitions, even when I squeezed in hydration and paperwork around QFC. The light rail closure between Westlake and University District didn’t hit me directly since my route was eastside/downtown, but it still made me mentally double-check timing whenever I was headed toward Pike Place. The USA vs Wales match gave me a kind of social reset; watching with my friend let me vent excitement without thinking about anything heavy. By the time I played basketball and showered, I felt grounded instead of drained.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
